intro: Oeosporangium × malacitense is a rare and unique species of lichen, a composite organism consisting of fungi and algae in symbiosis, commonly found in mountainous regions. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various skin conditions and infections. The bioactive constituents of Oeosporangium × malacitense include usnic acid, a compound with known antioxidant and antifungal properties, as well as other secondary metabolites that contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare topical creams, ointments, and extracts, which are used to treat skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and wounds, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
